page: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-3840s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor operating at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs, resulting in a thread-per-core ratio of 1.0. Notably, the CPU allocation is shared rather than dedicated. The processor is equipped with 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AES, SHA_NI, BMI2, and FMA.

The server is provisioned with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 3840 GB NVMe SSD. The network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. There are no dedicated GPU resources associated with this server type.

In synthetic benchmarks, the server demonstrates divergent performance characteristics. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 2912.97 is rated as strong, placing it in the top 25% of tested instances. Conversely, the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 46204.81 falls into the average category, representing the middle 50%. This multi-core performance profile reflects the shared CPU allocation tradeoff, where resource contention may affect sustained multi-threaded workloads despite the high physical core count.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high density of memory and local NVMe SSD storage relative to its shared CPU compute class. This makes it suitable for workloads that require substantial memory capacity and fast local storage but do not demand continuous, dedicated multi-core CPU performance. Appropriate workloads include memory-intensive databases, caching layers, large-scale file processing, and general-purpose applications with bursty CPU demands.
description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-3840s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores on an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB per core, and includes a bundled 3840 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. In benchmark testing, the server achieved a strong stress-ng single-core score of 2912.97, placing it in the top 25%, while its multi-core score of 46204.81 is average. This resource profile offers high memory and storage density relative to its shared compute class, presenting a qualitative tradeoff for multi-threaded workloads. It is suited for memory-intensive databases, caching systems, and general-purpose applications with bursty CPU requirements.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-3840s-amd is an x86_64 server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 3840 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core and average multi-core benchmark performance.

tagline: An x86_64 instance pairing 128 GB of RAM and 3.8 TB of NVMe storage with shared AMD EPYC compute cores.
